findings.evidence: `DegreeSplitting.lean:118-121` gives `degreeCorrection_apply`, and `Principal.lean:234-236` gives `weightedDegreeClass_divisorClass`, so the divisor-class statement is immediate and strictly more natural than the point-only proof at lines 56-75.
findings.fix: First add the general lemma `S.degreeCorrection w h x₀ (S.divisorClass D) = S.divisorClass (D - weightedDegree w D • ofPoint x₀)` with no `hx₀`, then derive the point/Abel-Jacobi lemma from it.
findings.issue: `degreeCorrection_divisorClass_ofPoint` proves only the point case and carries the base-point weight assumption through the statement, although degree correction of any divisor class has the same formal shape.

findings.evidence: Lines 86-88 already prove `degreeCorrection_coe_picZero` for arbitrary `p : picZero w h`, but lines 109-118 specialize the product-splitting consequence to `S.weightedAbelJacobiClass ... x`.
findings.fix: Replace/factor through `@[simp] lemma classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt_coe_picZero ... (p : picZero w h) : S.classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt w h hx₀ (p : S.ClassGroup) = (p, 0)` and similarly for `unweightedPicZero`; derive the Abel-Jacobi lemmas as special cases if desired.
findings.issue: The splitting formula for a coerced Pic⁰ class is stated only for `weightedAbelJacobiClass`, and the unweighted analogue at line 170 repeats that special case.

summary: The new compatibility lemmas are mostly at the intended weighted level, but two point/Abel-Jacobi statements should be factored through natural general lemmas for divisor classes and arbitrary Pic⁰ elements.
